blockingIssues.description: default_install_providers() returns vec![] in hermes-only builds (src/hooks/mod.rs:565). 'hermip hooks install' and 'hermip hooks install --all' do nothing useful when only the hermes feature is enabled. Hermes is never in the default install list, which is inconsistent with default_uninstall_providers() which correctly includes HookProvider::Hermes in all cfg variants. Fix: include HookProvider::Hermes in default_install_providers() for the hermes-only case (and arguably all cases since Hermes is always a valid provider).

Hardcoded ingress message (src/hooks/mod.rs:36) lists all provider names '<codex|claude-code|hermes>' regardless of feature flags. In a hermes-only build, this incorrectly tells the user that codex and claude-code are valid --provider values. Should be feature-flag-aware via cfg-gated alternative println! statements or a runtime-constructed provider list.

Slack route validation rejects valid configs where defaults.webhook_slack is set but no route-level webhook is configured (src/config.rs:937). The validation `if !has_slack_webhook` does not check `self.defaults.webhook_slack.is_some()`, so a config using the new default webhook fallback will fail daemon startup validation. The router (src/router.rs:312-318) correctly falls back to defaults.webhook_slack, but daemon calls config.validate() before starting (src/daemon.rs:48). Fix: change `if !has_slack_webhook` to `if !has_slack_webhook && self.defaults.webhook_slack.is_none()`. Add test validation_passes_with_default_webhook_slack.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md to clarify Slack resilience differences from Discord: Slack uses HTTP Retry-After header (Discord uses JSON body), Slack retries non-429 errors (Discord does not). Added resilience parity note that constants are identical but Slack is strictly more resilient for non-429 errors.
Added test-features command to services.yaml for testing feature flag combinations (hermes-only, hermes+codex, hermes+claude, all-features). Covers 4 key build configurations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add feature flag conventions: (1) New provider variants must be gated behind feature flags in Cargo.toml with #[cfg(feature = "...")] attributes on enum variants, constants, and code paths. (2) When gating providers, ensure default_install_providers() and default_uninstall_providers() are consistent across cfg variants — every cfg variant must have at least one installable provider. (3) CLI help text and runtime messages should be feature-flag-aware, not hardcoded. (4) Test key feature combinations before committing: hermes-only, hermes+one-provider, all-features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: hook-provider-feature-flags review found default_install_providers() returns empty vec in hermes-only builds and ingress message hardcodes all provider names regardless of features. These are cfg-related bugs that conventions could prevent.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the 'Design Decisions' section pattern for ARCHITECTURE.md: When an implementation choice diverges from the initial specification (e.g., permissive vs strict validation), workers should add a '## Design Decisions' section to ARCHITECTURE.md with subheaders per decision, rationale points, and implications. This pattern is now established (unknown source handling decision) but not documented as a convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: source-validation-design review noted the Design Decisions section pattern is established but not mentioned in AGENTS.md or skill files. Future workers might scatter rationale in code comments instead.

rejectedObservations.observation: The `with<F: FnMut(&str) -> Option<String>>` injectable env reader pattern should be documented in library.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — environment.md already has an 'Injectable Env-Var Reader Pattern' section listing all four public _with methods and their purpose.

rejectedObservations.observation: The `deprecated_fallback()` pattern for CLAWHIP_* backward compat should be documented.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — env-var-migration.md already documents the deprecated fallback pattern comprehensively with testing examples and all CLAWHIP_* mappings.

rejectedObservations.observation: The `config_with_toml_values()` test helper should be documented in library.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — too specific for library documentation. env-var-migration.md already provides testing patterns. The helper is internal to config.rs and may change.

rejectedObservations.observation: Two deprecation helpers (deprecated_fallback injectable vs env_var_non_empty_deprecated direct) with different testability should be unified.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — this requires a design decision about whether all env-dependent code should use the injectable pattern. Both work correctly. Not blocking for foundation milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: No centralized deprecation logging facility — eprintln! used at 8+ call sites with no shared macro.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — would require an architecture decision about whether to introduce a deprecation logging abstraction. Current approach works correctly. Not blocking.

rejectedObservations.observation: Retry orchestration code is duplicated between Discord and Slack sinks.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — each sink has API-specific differences (Discord parses JSON body retry_after, Slack parses HTTP header; Discord has bot+webhook clients, Slack has only webhook; Slack has exponential backoff for non-429). A shared abstraction would need to handle these differences. Current duplication is an accepted trade-off.

rejectedObservations.observation: Valid event kind prefixes should be a structured reference table in library.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — architecture.md already lists valid prefixes in the Design Decisions section with full descriptions and testing notes.

rejectedObservations.observation: CLAWHIP_* compat layer pattern should be documented in AGENTS.md.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — environment.md and env-var-migration.md already document the naming convention, fallback pattern, and deprecation warnings. Adding to AGENTS.md would be redundant with existing library docs.
